# Update Mask

Update commands' update mask.

## Proto Requirements [AIP-161](https://google.aip.dev/161):
* Users should be able to use an updateMask to determine which field needs
to be updated.

```
// The request structure for the UpdateResource method.
message UpdateResourceRequest {
  // The desired state for the resource.
  .update_mask.v1.Resource resource = 1
      [(.google.api.field_behavior) = REQUIRED];

  ...

  // Field mask is used to specify the fields to be overwritten in the
  // Resource resource by the update.
  //
  // The fields specified in the update_mask are relative to the resource, not
  // the full request. A field will be overwritten if it is in the mask.
  //
  // If the user does not provide a mask then all fields in the
  // Resource will be overwritten.
  .google.protobuf.FieldMask update_mask = 5;
}
```

## Gcloud Config:
* Autogenerated commands can default the update mask to _*_ which means all
fields are updated.

```
apis:
- name: UpdateMask
  supports_star_update_masks: false
```

## YAML Output:
* Autogenerated commands always have read_modify_update set to True. This
enables gcloud to make a GET request for the resource before updating the
resource. This ensure values are not wiped out but is also makes updating
list and maps through special flags possible.
* Gcloud will try to automatically generate an update mask based on the values
provided. However, it will provide a `*` value if updateMask is specified as
a static field.

```
request:
  api_version: v1
  collection:
  - updatemask.projects.locations.resources
  <!-- '*' if supports_star_update_masks if set to True -->
  static_fields:
    updateMask: '*'
update:
  read_modify_update: true
```

## Gcloud UX:
* Update mask is always provided through gcloud and never specified on the
command line.

```
$ gcloud update-mask resources update ...
```
